title: Root Layout Component (`app/layout.tsx`)
content:
This file defines the root layout for the DocMed application, serving as the main shell for all pages. It sets up global styles, font imports, and integrates with Clerk for authentication. ### Purpose The primary purpose of this file is to establish the foundational structure of the web application. It ensures that common elements like the HTML doctype, ``, and `` tags are present, and applies global styling and font configurations. It also wraps the entire application in `ClerkProvider`, enabling authentication features across the site. ### Key Features & Exports * **Metadata Definition (`metadata`)**: Configures the title and description for the application, which are used by search engines and browser tabs. * `title`: "DocMed — Self-Healing Documentation" * `description`: "AI-powered documentation that monitors your codebase and heals itself when code changes. End doc rot forever." * **Font Imports**: Imports `Geist` and `Geist_Mono` fonts from `next/font/google` and assigns them CSS variables (`--font-geist-sans`, `--font-geist-mono`) for use in global styling. * **Clerk Provider (`ClerkProvider`)**: Wraps the entire application to provide authentication context and services from Clerk. * **Root Layout Component (`RootLayout`)**: This is the main React component that renders the application's layout. It accepts `children` as a prop, which represents the content of the current page. * Applies global CSS classes for styling (`h-full`, `antialiased`, `dark`). * Includes the imported fonts via CSS variables. * Sets up the `` with basic styling (`min-h-full`, `flex`, `flex-col`). ### Usage Example This component is automatically used by Next.js for all routes. Developers do not need to explicitly import or use `RootLayout` in other files. Any content placed within the `app` directory (e.g., `app/page.tsx`, `app/dashboard/page.tsx`) will be rendered as `children` within this layout. ```typescript // app/page.tsx (Example of content rendered within the layout) export default function HomePage() { return (

Welcome to DocMed!

Your AI-powered documentation solution.

); } ``` When `app/page.tsx` is rendered, its content will be placed inside the `` tag, and the global styles and fonts defined in `app/layout.tsx` will be applied.
